p1_string_of_nickels.physical_facts: Located in yellow pot; dense foliage cluster; leaves remain turgid with consistent green pigmentation.
p1_string_of_nickels.explanatory_transformations: Stable throughout the 5-day sequence. No significant morphological changes observed.
p1_string_of_nickels.visual_health_inference: Healthy. The plant shows no signs of wilting or chlorosis, indicating successful adaptation to current environmental conditions.

p2_mexican_mint.physical_facts: Located in black pot; two primary leaves visible with smaller growth emerging.
p2_mexican_mint.explanatory_transformations: The plant has transitioned from a state of visible drooping (T-5) to a more stabilized posture following the application of starch water.
p2_mexican_mint.visual_health_inference: Recovering. The reduction in leaf margin dehydration suggests the starch water application has provided necessary hydration support.

p3_pothos.physical_facts: Two leaves present; white rabbit scale anchor (5cm) positioned near the base.
p3_pothos.explanatory_transformations: The leaf tip necrosis observed in the earliest image has remained static, indicating no further tissue degradation.
p3_pothos.visual_health_inference: Stable. The plant is maintaining its current physiological state despite VPD stress.

p4_silver_guest.physical_facts: Smallest specimen, located near the rim of the black pot shared with p2.
p4_silver_guest.explanatory_transformations: Shows slight improvement in leaf turgidity compared to the T-5 baseline.
p4_silver_guest.visual_health_inference: Improving. The plant is responding positively to the supplementary starch water.

biome_observations.soil_surface: Presence of white starch residue confirms the human action of 'supplementary_starch_water' application. Soil moisture appears adequate across all pots.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no debris or fungal growth detected.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: The sequence shows a clear progression from initial dehydration (T-5) to stabilization (Current) following the user's intervention on 2026-04-09.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a state of recovery. The starch water application has successfully mitigated the acute dehydration observed in p2 and p4.
vision_report.anomalies: None. The white material on the soil is confirmed as the starch residue from the user's recent action.
vision_report.narrative_description: The garden has transitioned from a period of stress to a stabilized state. The starch water application has effectively addressed the hydration deficits noted in the Mexican Mint and Silver Guest. The String of Nickels and Pothos remain stable, showing high resilience to the ongoing VPD conditions.
